summary: Tested Apple Pay / Google Pay integration (Stripe). Domain association file OK, Checkout frontend loads without runtime errors and WalletPaymentButton hides itself on desktop chromium (no wallet) — as expected. CRITICAL: /api/kiosk/book returns HTTP 502 because Stripe API rejects `automatic_payment_methods` on Checkout Session with error 'Received unknown parameter: automatic_payment_methods'.

critical.issue: Stripe returns 400 'Received unknown parameter: automatic_payment_methods' → backend returns 502 'Payment service unavailable'. Booking flow is BROKEN. Reproduced with payload {hotelSlug: 'bristol', destination: 'CDG Airport', date: '2026-08-15', time: '10:00', vehicleType: 'sedan', price: 95, clientName: 'TEST User', clientPhone: '+33612345678'}. Stripe log: 'error_code=parameter_unknown error_param=automatic_payment_methods'. Root cause: the Stripe account's pinned API version is older than 2023-08-16 (when automatic_payment_methods was added to Checkout Sessions), and stripe-python 14.3.0 does NOT override that pin. Fix options: (a) upgrade the account's default API version in Stripe Dashboard → Developers → API version; (b) set `stripe.api_version = '2024-06-20'` (or later) in routes/kiosk.py; (c) simplest: REMOVE the automatic_payment_methods param entirely (and don't set payment_method_types) — Checkout will fall back to Dashboard-configured payment methods, which already include Apple Pay & Google Pay once the domain is verified. This is critical because it also blocks the pre-existing Kiosk booking flow, which is now a regression.

checkout_frontend_load.page_errors: none (only pre-existing 403 on a favicon-like resource; unrelated)
checkout_frontend_load.status: PASS — WalletPaymentButton import does not break the page, and correctly renders null when wallet unsupported

wallet_button_component_review.note: Cannot test the actual Apple Pay / Google Pay UI in Chromium (browser has neither wallet). PaymentRequestButtonElement iframe only mounts when canMakePayment() resolves truthy.

critical_code_review_comments: routes/kiosk.py:362 — Even if automatic_payment_methods worked, please also pass currency at the session level or verify — currently ok because it is inside line_items.price_data. No change needed.

routes/kiosk.py — dispatch_to_csharp has hardcoded creds `Nandetiri1@gmail.com/12345678` (line 495). Please move to env vars — same secret is already loaded elsewhere via CSHARP_COMPANY_USERNAME/PASSWORD.

Checkout.js:945 — the wallet onPaymentMethod handler reads `localStorage.getItem('token')` but the rest of the file uses `localStorage.getItem('auth_token')`. This will send an EMPTY Authorization header for the SetupIntent call in the wallet flow → 401. Fix: change to 'auth_token'.

Checkout.js:947 — wallet flow calls POST /api/proxy/setup-intent/create while the card flow (line 562) calls POST /api/proxy/booking/setup-intent. Confirm the /setup-intent/create endpoint exists on backend, otherwise wallet payment will fail with 404 even when a device supports Apple/Google Pay.
action_items: URGENT: Fix Kiosk 502. Recommended: replace `automatic_payment_methods={'enabled': True}` with either (a) `stripe.api_version = '2024-06-20'` set once at module load AND keep the param, or (b) simply remove BOTH `automatic_payment_methods` and `payment_method_types` so Stripe uses Dashboard-configured methods (this is Stripe's official recommendation for Checkout).
Fix Checkout.js wallet onPaymentMethod: use `localStorage.getItem('auth_token')` (not 'token') so the SetupIntent call is authenticated.

Verify (or create) backend endpoint `/api/proxy/setup-intent/create` — Checkout wallet flow calls it but the card flow uses `/api/proxy/booking/setup-intent`. Endpoint mismatch will silently break Apple/Google Pay on Checkout.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (page loads, component renders null as expected)

context_for_next_testing_agent: Kiosk booking is currently blocked by Stripe API rejecting automatic_payment_methods. Reproduce with: curl -X POST $URL/api/kiosk/book -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"hotelSlug":"bristol","clientName":"TEST","clientPhone":"+33612345678","destination":"CDG Airport","destinationAddress":"CDG","date":"2026-08-15","time":"10:00","vehicleType":"sedan","price":95}'. Stripe log confirms: 'error_code=parameter_unknown error_param=automatic_payment_methods'. The frontend Checkout page loaded fine and WalletPaymentButton import did NOT crash the module — good regression signal. Two Checkout.js wallet-flow bugs also flagged in critical_code_review_comments (wrong localStorage key + endpoint mismatch).
